design_summary: Round 2 of the bounded candidate-language programme. Two justified families plus an agnostic random control, each a BOUNDED preregistered correspondence (phoneme inventory + allowed changes into the LA syllabary) + a SMALL cited lexicon of candidate-language WORDS. Each lexeme is expanded to its predicted LA syllabogram-value string(s) via the FIXED correspondence (never read off LA). A held-out LA multi-sign word MATCHES a family iff its value-string is a predicted whole-word form, or (for affix entries) it ends with a predicted affix string. Primary statistic = token-weighted match rate on the held-out multi-sign syllabic word set. Scored vs the WP6 multi-family end-to-end null: (S) order-shuffle, (W) wrong-language opaque LB length-matched, (R) random-prior (lexicon permuted, search size preserved). Decisive nulls for a LANGUAGE claim = W and R. FWER per family = fraction of decisive null realizations with match >= real. Search-adjusted across the families by Holm. Leave-one-lexeme-out reported. 300-draw random-lexicon false-clear calibration.
non_circularity: Correspondences map candidate-language PHONEMES to syllabogram VALUES; predicted forms are DERIVED from candidate-language dictionary words, not fitted to LA. LB values only ever appear as the wrong-language NULL corpus. The conventional LA transliteration is a fixed published convention applied identically to every corpus and family.
bounded_no_root_search: Lexicons are fixed a priori (6 entries/family), from cited proposals; no unbounded thesaurus search. The combinatorial expansion size (effective_n predicted forms) is reported and is automatically deflated because every null realization runs the SAME expansion/matcher.
syllabogram_decomposition_note: Each AB syllabogram is decomposed to (onset_consonant, vowel) by standard Ventris-Chadwick values in the runner. Pure-vowel signs have empty onset.

HUR_Hurrian.gloss: Hurro-Urartian (Hurrian). Sources: Wegner, Hurritisch: Eine Einfuhrung (2000); Wilhelm, The Hurrians (1989); the recurring Aegean-Hurrian contact proposals. Ergative agglutinating language; voiceless/voiced stop allophony -> both mapped to the plain LA series. Common admin/kinship/title lexemes derived through the fixed coarse correspondence.

LUW2_Luwian.gloss: Luwian / Anatolian-2 (distinct from round-1's broad pre-Greek/Luwic ANA). Sources: Melchert, Cuneiform Luvian Lexicon (1993); Yakubovich, Sociolinguistics of the Luvian Language (2010). Vowel system a/i/u (no e/o) reflected in the vowel_map. Core divine/kinship/administrative lexemes and the -assa genitival adjective, derived through the fixed correspondence.

lexicon.phonemes: m a s s a n a

CTRL_agnostic_random_r2.gloss: Agnostic negative control: a fixed random lexicon of matched size/length-structure, no linguistic motivation. Expected FWER ~ uniform; verifies the pipeline is not rigged to pass. If this control clears the raw bar, no round-2 family can COUNT.
CTRL_agnostic_random_r2.lexicon: GENERATED at runtime: 6 random entries (4 word + 2 suffix) with lengths matched to the round-2 real families, phonemes drawn C/V-alternating from this inventory under fixed seed 20260708.

held_out_target: GORILA word units, syllabic-only, multi-sign (>=2 signs): 973 tokens. Single-sign words excluded (non-discriminating for lexical hypotheses).
primary_statistic: token-weighted match rate = sum token-freq(matching words)/total tokens, on the multi-sign held-out set.
decision_rule: A family COUNTS iff Holm-adjusted decisive FWER < 0.05 AND leave-one-lexeme-out worst decisive FWER < 0.05 AND it beats the random-lexicon calibration (real percentile p<0.05, false-clear rate <=0.10) AND the agnostic control does NOT clear the raw bar.
prior_expectation: Round 1 was AT_END_TO_END_NULL (wrong-language LB scored higher; West Semitic 'cleared' 0.030 but so did the random control -> artifact). Honest expectation for round 2 is again AT_END_TO_END_NULL; run properly and report which family, if any, COUNTS.
